text: Lesson 47
zh: 第47课

text: The great escape
zh: 大逃亡

text: What is one of the features of modern camping where nationality is concerned?
zh: 就国籍而言，现代露营的一个特点是什么？

text: Economy is one powerful motive for camping,
zh: 经济实惠是露营的一大动力，

text: since after the initial outlay upon equipment, or through hiring it, the total expense can be far less than the cost of hotels.
zh: 因为除了最初购置装备（或租用装备）的花费外，总费用可能远低于住旅馆的开支。

text: But, contrary to a popular assumption, it is far from being the only one, or even the greatest.
zh: 但是，与普遍的看法相反，这远非唯一的原因，甚至也不是最主要的原因。

text: The man who manoeuvres carelessly into his 20 pounds' worth of space at one of Europe's myriad permanent sites may find himself bumping a Bentley.
zh: 在欧洲无数的固定营地中，若有人不小心把车挪进自己那价值20英镑的场地，可能会一头撞上一辆宾利。

text: More likely, Ford Escort will be hub to hub with Renault or Mercedes, but rarely with bicycles made for two.
zh: 更常见的情况是，福特Escort的轮毂会紧挨着雷诺或奔驰的轮毂，却很少会挨着双人自行车。

text: That the equipment of modern camping becomes yearly more sophisticated is an entertaining paradox for the cynic,
zh: 现代露营装备逐年愈发精良，这对愤世�ko者来说是个有趣的悖论，

text: a brighter promise for the hopeful traveller who has sworn to get away from it all.
zh: 而对那些一心想要彻底逃离尘嚣的满怀希望的旅行者来说，则是更光明的前景。

text: It also provides--and some student sociologist might care to base his thesis upon the phenomenon--an escape of another kind.
zh: 露营还提供了——某位研究社会学的学生或许可以拿这一现象作论文题目——另一种意义上的逃离。

text: The modern traveller is often a man who dislikes the Splendide and the Bellavista,
zh: 现代旅行者常常是那种厌恶豪华酒店和高档度假胜地的人，

text: not because he cannot afford, or shuns their material comforts, but because he is afraid of them.
zh: 这并非因为他们负担不起，也不是嫌弃那里的物质享受，而是因为他们对此心存畏惧。

text: Affluent he may be, but he is by no means sure what to tip the doorman or the chambermaid.
zh: 他或许很富有，但他并不清楚该给门童或女服务员多少小费。

text: Master in his own house he has little idea of when to say boo to a manager hotel.
zh: 在自己家中他是一家之主，可一旦到了酒店，他却不知该在什么时候对经理说“不”。

text: From all such fears camping releases him.
zh: 露营让他摆脱了这一切担忧。

text: Granted, a snobbery of camping itself, based upon equipment and techniques, already exists;
zh: 诚然，露营界本身也存在一种以装备和技巧为基础的势利风气；

text: but it is of a kind that, if he meets it, he can readily understand and deal with.
zh: 但这种风气，即便他遇到了，也很容易理解和应付。

text: There is no superior 'they' in the shape of managements and hotel hierarchies to darken his holiday days.
zh: 这里没有那种以管理层和酒店等级制度形式存在、高高在上的“他们”来给他的假期蒙上阴影。

text: To such motives, yet another must be added.
zh: 除了上述动机之外，还必须加上另一个。

text: The contemporary phenomenon of car worship is to be explained not least by the sense of independence and freedom that ownership entails.
zh: 当代人对汽车的崇拜，很大程度上可以用拥有汽车所带来的独立感和自由感来解释。

text: To this pleasure camping gives an exquisite refinement.
zh: 而露营则为这种乐趣增添了一种精致的升华。

text: From one's own front door to home or foreign hills or sands and back again, everything is to hand.
zh: 从自家门口出发，无论是去本国还是异国的山峦或沙滩，再返回家中，一切都触手可及。

text: Not only are the means of arriving at the holiday paradise entirely within one's own command and keeping,
zh: 抵达假日天堂的手段完全掌握在自己手中，

text: but the means of escape from holiday hell (if the beach proves too crowded, the local weather too inclement)
zh: 而逃离假日地狱的手段（如果海滩人满为患，或当地天气恶劣）

text: are there, outside--or, as likely, part of--the tent.
zh: 也就在那里——就在帐篷外面，甚至常常就是帐篷的一部分。

text: Idealists have objected to the practice of camping, as to the package tour,
zh: 理想主义者曾像批评跟团旅游一样批评露营，

text: that the traveller abroad thereby denies himself the opportunity of getting to know the people of the country visited.
zh: 认为这样一来，出国旅行者便剥夺了自己了解所访国家民众的机会。

text: Insularity and self-containment, it is argued, go hand in hand.
zh: 他们认为，孤立与自我封闭是相伴而生的。

text: The opinion does not survive experience of a popular Continental camping place.
zh: 然而只要体验过一个热门的欧洲大陆露营地，这种看法便站不住脚了。

text: Holiday hotels tend to cater for one nationality of visitors especially, sometimes exclusively.
zh: 度假酒店往往专门迎合某一国籍的游客，有时甚至只招待这一国籍的客人。

text: Camping sites, by contrast, are highly cosmopolitan.
zh: 相比之下，露营地则高度国际化。

text: Granted, a preponderance of Germans is a characteristic that seems common to most Mediterranean sites;
zh: 诚然，德国人占多数似乎是大多数地中海营地的共同特征；

text: but as yet there is no overwhelmingly specialized patronage.
zh: 但目前为止，还没有哪个营地是被某一国游客彻底垄断的。

text: Notices forbidding the open-air drying of clothes,
zh: 那些禁止露天晾晒衣物、

text: or the use of water points for car washing,
zh: 或禁止用取水点洗车的告示，

text: or those inviting 'our camping friends' to a dance or a boat trip are printed not only in French or Italian or Spanish, but also in English, German and Dutch.
zh: 以及邀请“露营的朋友们”参加舞会或乘船游览的通知，不仅用法语、意大利语或西班牙语印刷，还用英语、德语和荷兰语印刷。

text: At meal times the odour of sauerkraut vies with that of garlic.
zh: 用餐时分，酸菜的气味与大蒜的气味相互较量。

text: The Frenchman's breakfast coffee competes with the Englishman's bacon and eggs.
zh: 法国人的早餐咖啡与英国人的培根煎蛋分庭抗礼。

text: Whether the remarkable growth of organized camping means the eventual death of the more independent kind is hard to say.
zh: 有组织的露营活动显著增长，这是否意味着更为自由独立的露营方式终将消亡，很难说。

text: Municipalities naturally want to secure the campers' site fees and other custom.
zh: 市政当局自然希望能确保收取露营者的场地费及其他消费。

text: Police are wary of itinerants who cannot be traced to a recognized camp boundary or to four walls.
zh: 警方则对那些无法追溯到正规营地边界或固定住所的流浪者心存警惕。

text: But most probably it will all depend upon campers themselves: how many heath fires they cause; how much litter they leave;
zh: 但最有可能的是，这一切最终取决于露营者自身：他们引发了多少荒野火灾，留下了多少垃圾；

text: in short, whether or not they wholly alienate landowners and those who live in the countryside.
zh: 简而言之，就是他们是否会彻底激怒地主和当地居民。

text: Only good scouting is likely to preserve the freedoms so dear to the heart of the eternal Boy Scout.
zh: 唯有良好的野外守则，才有望保住那令永远的童子军心心念念的自由。
